texto: If our goal is to slow migration, then the best way to do so is to work for a more equitable global system. But slowing migration is an odd goal, if the real problem is global inequality.
explicacao: Citação de Aviva Chomsky sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “If our goal is to slow migration, then the best way to do so is to work for a more equitable glob…” articula motivacao, immigration, inequality em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como trabalho e inequality.

frase_original: If our goal is to slow migration, then the best way to do so is to work for a more equitable global system. But slowing migration is an odd goal, if the real problem is global inequality.

texto: Like other discriminatory legislation in our country's history, immigration laws define and differentiate legal status on the basis of arbitrary attributes. Immigration laws create unequal rights. People who break immigration laws don't cause harm or even potential harm (unlike, for example, drunk driving, which creates the potential for harm even if no accident occurs). Rather, people who break immigration laws do things that are perfectly legal for others, but denied to them--like crossing a border or, even more commonly, simply exist.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Aviva Chomsky: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Like other discriminatory legislation in our country's history, immigration laws define and diffe…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, borders, discrimination pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de estudos e discrimination.
